body.title: Inference router increment 5 — reconciled escalation driver (one within-domain difficulty-walk, DS-driven, ends in system_alarm)
body.intention: I intend that a mechanical inference failure bumps the call's difficulty one bucket and re-routes through the SAME selector, so capability-escalation is one mechanism reusing the existing dispatch loop — not a parallel fork.
body.description:
**Problem:** the cost-optimizing selector is STATELESS — it picks the cheapest capable source for a difficulty, but does not retry on failure. Three HALF-built escalation mechanisms exist in parallel and none is live: (a) device.py's escalation_hop ceiling/prior-attempt-prepend is NEVER incremented in production; (b) dicksimnel/device.py's _TIER_CASCADE is a degenerate single tier; (c) DS._run_inference ad-hoc appends a prior-attempt note to the ticket description. The epic wants ONE mechanism: 'on capability failure, bump difficulty one bucket and re-run the SAME selector'.

**RESOLVED (D-inference-domain-routing-2026-07-01 — supersedes the NEEDS-AKIEN block below):** Akien set the driver + endpoint policy.
- **Driver location = DS._run_inference (caller-side).** device.py's dispatch runs ONE call and structurally cannot observe 'did-not-reach-DONE' (a ToolLoop terminal). So DS._run_inference is the live driver: it already sees all four ToolLoop terminals. On a CAPABILITY failure it re-dispatches with escalation_hop+1 and the required difficulty bucket bumped one rung, re-running the same (now domain-aware) selector. This finally makes escalation_hop a LIVE, provable driver — not the dead code that blocked this ticket.
- **Availability vs capability (the decision's own words: 'Hex-DOWN is not a branch').** A call that RAISED / timed out / source-down is NOT escalation — the source is simply absent from candidates and the selector's next-cheapest wins at the SAME difficulty; a separate system_alarm fires for the down. ONLY a reached-a-terminal-but-never-DONE (MAX_TURNS / stall — the ToolLoop terminals visible only to DS) is a CAPABILITY failure that bumps difficulty.
- **Endpoint CHANGE (explicit — do NOT preserve old behavior).** Today every DS escalation endpoint returns-to-CC (~$0). That return-to-CC endpoint is REMOVED. On capability failure the walk auto-escalates UP to the top cloud tier for the domain (paid, UNATTENDED — Akien blessed this: 'we've escalated to the top tier model in the cloud for this task, and say it can''t be done. inference failure. system alarm.'). Only when the TOP difficulty tier for the domain ALSO fails -> inference failure -> system_alarm -> HALT that ticket for analysis. Clean terminal, no loop.
- **Domain-aware.** The bumped re-run stays in the SAME domain (T-inference-domain-tag); only the difficulty rung rises. Prompt stays the domain's prompt (T-inference-domain-prompt).

**Proposed shape:** (1) In DS._run_inference, on a capability terminal (MAX_TURNS/stall/non-DONE, NOT a raised/timeout availability failure), set escalation_hop+=1, bump the required difficulty one rung, populate prior_attempt, and re-dispatch through the selector. (2) COLLAPSE the three half-mechanisms into this one path: drive device.py's existing escalation_hop ceiling(2)+prior-attempt-prepend from here; retire the degenerate _TIER_CASCADE; fold DS's ad-hoc description-append into prior_attempt. (3) rules_engine.route() gains an explicit required-difficulty override param so the walk can request a harder bucket. (4) Terminate: bump past the top tier -> terminal routing error + system_alarm + halt ticket. NO quality judge (deferred). **Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/device.py (_run_inference driver + retire _TIER_CASCADE), devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py (terminal classification availability-vs-capability), devices/inference/device.py (escalation_hop path now actually driven), devices/inference/rules_engine.py (required-difficulty override). **Design rules:** ALL inference through the Proxy; NO SQLITE; log every escalation hop (state change) at the crossing; log the availability-vs-capability classification. **Scope boundary:** the capability-failure->bump->re-run walk + availability-vs-capability split + termination + collapse of the three mechanisms into one. OUT: quality-judge escalation; the domain tag/prompt themselves (T-inference-domain-tag / -prompt); the cost record (T-inference-cost-learn-verify). **Test plan (stub-first red->green):** (1) a CAPABILITY failure (MAX_TURNS with tool calls) at the worker tier re-dispatches at the next difficulty tier with escalation_hop=1; (2) an AVAILABILITY failure (call raised/timeout) does NOT bump difficulty — it re-selects next-cheapest at the SAME tier (or system_alarms if none), no hop increment; (3) bump past the top tier -> clean terminal failure + system_alarm, NO loop, ticket halted; (4) a successful first pick does NOT escalate; (5) only ONE escalation mechanism remains — no parallel _TIER_CASCADE, no hop double-counting, old return-to-CC endpoint gone. **Completion criteria:** capability failure bumps difficulty one rung and re-runs the domain-aware selector with escalation_hop+1; availability failure re-selects at same difficulty without a hop; termination past the top tier is a clean system_alarm + halt; the three half-mechanisms are collapsed into one; tests green.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: One escalation driver shipped: DS._run_inference walks difficulty UP one rung per CAPABILITY failure (self-ESCALATE/MAX_TURNS/prose-no-DONE → escalation_hop+1 → cost-ordered selector picks cheapest-capable at the harder tier, Ollama/Google before Anthropic); AVAILABILITY failure (dispatch raised OR no-source error response → None) re-selects next-cheapest at SAME difficulty, bounded (2 retries) then system_alarm — never bumps to paid on a source-down; past-top rung → system_alarm + clean halt, checked BEFORE re-dispatch so no loop; COST_EXCEEDED → halt. Collapsed the 3 half-mechanisms (device escalation_hop now driven, _TIER_CASCADE retired, ad-hoc append folded into prior_attempt); return-to-CC endpoint gone. bump_difficulty + route(required_difficulty=) override + escalation_hop→difficulty map. Fresh adversarial reviewer confirmed availability CANNOT drive paid escalation and termination is loop-free; found the guard line was untested → added inversion-verified + neuter-verified regression tests. Commits b081d4d9 + 90552b04. 0 new regressions (DS 70 passed).
body.unproven_reason: The STATIC money-safety property is now genuinely proven (money-safety terminals pinned by tests proven non-vacuous via logic-inversion; the load-bearing no-source→None guard pinned via neutering — a hollow build fails both). The remaining lever is the BEHAVIORAL proof Akien named: run LIVE DS tickets one at a time against Hex and read the per-call cost_records to confirm the walk spends what the classification predicts (paid only on genuine capability ceilings, cost-ordered Ollama/Google-before-Anthropic). That needs DS online (Hex delivery + DS cost constraint lifted) — the automatic per-ticket cost TOTAL to watch it by is T-inference-ledger-domain-tier-columns. Also the additive-test proof_emitter gap (T-proof-emitter-additive-surface-mode).
